game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Spelunky 2 builds upon the unique, randomized challenges that made the original a roguelike classic, offering a huge adventure designed to satisfy players old and new. Meet the next generation of explorers as they find themselves on the Moon, searching for treasure and missing family.

Spelunky returns in this greatly-expanded sequel to the original roguelike platformer! Meet the next generation of explorers as they find themselves on the Moon, searching for treasure and missing family. Spelunky 2 builds upon the unique, randomized challenges that made the original a classic, offering a huge adventure designed to satisfy players old and new.

A Bigger and More Dynamic World, with So Much to See and Do


Spelunky 2's world is even denser than the one in the original game, offering many more areas, characters, traps, and items, as well as new ways to interact with them (and for them to interact with each other). The world has expanded in other ways, too, with branching paths and multi-layered levels adding a third dimension to the classic 2d platforming. Animals can also be tamed and ridden. And dynamic liquid physics create new challenges and make the world feel extra reactive and alive.

Build a Community, Make Friends


Back in Base Camp, characters you unlock will mill about and relax between runs, offering encouragement and advice. As you explore, your camp will expand and develop into a thriving little community to welcome you whenever you need a break from exploring. While there, you can also access shortcuts and practice your moves in safety.

The Game is Yours to Explore


Spelunky 2 is not just about "getting good and beating the game" - more than that, it's an endlessly replayable playground to experiment in and find your own way, creating stories against the backdrop of an expansive, deeply-interactive world filled with secrets. The more you play, the more the game becomes yours!

Key Features, Summarized:


  • A Bigger World with all new areas, monsters, traps, and items!
  • An Extra Dimension is added by allowing players to access a second layer behind the normal playfield. The second layer might contain treasure rooms, hidden passages, and other special places.
  • Multiple Routes Through the Game will add variety and give players more ways to personalize a run.
  • Find and Ride Mounts (like turkeys!) that have special abilities. Enemies may ride mounts, as well!
  • New NPCs that you can help, hurt, or ignore… to make each run feel even more like a unique story.
  • Variations and Changes to Classic Spelunky Concepts like shops and ghosts that will make veteran players rethink their strategies.
  • Improved Liquid Physics allow water and lava to move and pour realistically, which drastically affects the playfield (and looks cool)
  • Deadly Poison and Curses can be inflicted on players and enemies alike.
  • Build a Permanent Home at Base Camp as you do runs and explore the caves.
  • Improved Lighting and Special Effects are paired with richer visual and audio design.
  • Deeper Mysteries that may require a community effort to unravel.
  • Possibly More Eggplants
game_info.description: Explore dangerous procedurally-generated caves filled with treasures, traps, and enemies! As a daring spelunker, descend through multiple levels collecting gems and gold while avoiding deadly hazards. Use bombs to blast through obstacles and ropes to navigate carefully. Defeat or avoid monsters, discover secret areas, and try to reach the exit alive. Each run is unique with randomized layouts - how far can you delve into the depths?
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/Right: Move left and right
Arrow Up: Climb ladders, enter doors, use rope
Arrow Down: Crouch, descend through platforms
Space: Jump
Z: Throw bomb (if available)
Shift: Use rope (if available)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement, jumping, and gravity physics. Verifies that the player can navigate platforms, climb ladders, and respond correctly to keyboard inputs without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move left and right continuously, jump periodically to test physics, attempt to climb ladders when near them, and crouch on platforms. Test boundary collision with walls and floors.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player moves smoothly without clipping through walls, jumps work correctly with proper gravity, ladder climbing functions when positioned correctly, and no physics errors occur during 300+ frames of gameplay.

TEST_1.expected: Player moves smoothly without clipping through walls, jumps work correctly with proper gravity, ladder climbing functions when positioned correctly, and no physics errors occur during 300+ frames of gameplay.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests bomb mechanics including throwing, explosion timing, destruction of destructible tiles, and damage to enemies. Verifies item usage and inventory management.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Use bombs whenever available to destroy blocks and enemies. Test bomb throwing in different directions and verify explosions affect the correct area with proper timing.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Bombs explode after correct delay, destroy destructible blocks, damage enemies in blast radius, and inventory count decreases properly. No crashes or stuck states occur from bomb usage.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests rope mechanics including placement, climbing, and interaction with the environment. Validates that ropes help navigate vertical spaces correctly.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Deploy ropes when positioned over pits or needing vertical navigation. Attempt to climb ropes up and down, and verify proper collision with platforms while on ropes.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Ropes deploy correctly from player position, can be climbed smoothly, help traverse difficult areas, and inventory management works properly. No visual glitches or physics issues occur.

TEST_4.expected: Ropes deploy correctly from player position, can be climbed smoothly, help traverse difficult areas, and inventory management works properly. No visual glitches or physics issues occur.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests enemy AI behavior, collision with player, damage system, and death mechanics. Verifies that enemies patrol correctly and interact properly with the environment.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Allow enemies to patrol naturally while observing their movement patterns. Intentionally collide with enemies to test damage system. Verify enemies respect platform boundaries and ladders.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Enemies move in predictable patterns, deal damage on collision with player, respect environmental boundaries, and can be defeated by bombs or stomping. Player health decreases correctly and game over triggers at zero health.

TEST_6.test_description: Tests procedural level generation consistency, ensuring levels are solvable, properly connected, and contain appropriate density of obstacles, collectibles, and enemies.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Start multiple runs to generate different level layouts. Verify that each layout has a clear path to the exit, appropriate spacing of platforms, and balanced difficulty.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Each generated level is unique but solvable, contains the exit door, has proper platform connectivity, includes collectibles and enemies, and no unreachable areas or impossible jumps exist.
TEST_6.description: Tests procedural level generation consistency, ensuring levels are solvable, properly connected, and contain appropriate density of obstacles, collectibles, and enemies.

TEST_6.expected: Each generated level is unique but solvable, contains the exit door, has proper platform connectivity, includes collectibles and enemies, and no unreachable areas or impossible jumps exist.

TEST_7.test_description: Tests the complete game loop including start screen, gameplay, pause functionality, win/lose conditions, and restart mechanics. Validates proper phase transitions.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Progress through all game phases: start with ENTER, play normally, pause with ESC, trigger both win and lose conditions in separate runs, and restart with R.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: All phase transitions work smoothly, game state persists correctly during pause, win/lose screens display properly with accurate final scores, and restart returns to a clean start state.
